channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Moraes marca audiência de Eduardo para esclarecer sanções dos EUA ao Brasil
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes scheduled Eduardo Bolsonaro’s (PL-SP) interrogation for April 14 in a case where he is accused of obstruction of justice and coercion. The hearing aims to clarify U.S. sanctions imposed on Brazil related to the allegations against him.

item.title: Metade dos ministros deixa o governo, e Lula cobrará atuação em campanha eleitoral
item.description: President Lula convened his ministerial team to reinforce the need to defend government actions, particularly among ministers who will leave office to run in the October 2026 elections. The directive was aimed at encouraging these ministers to actively campaign in favor of the Workers' Party (PT).

item.title: Aliados de Castro veem intervenção do STF no RJ com manutenção de governador interino
item.description: Allies of former Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) view the STF's decision to maintain Justice Ricardo Couto as interim governor of Rio de Janeiro as a form of judicial intervention in the state. They argue that Couto's continued tenure until the election for the Guanabara Palace interferes with popular choice and represents an overreach of power. The measure was taken by the STF to ensure continuity of state administration until an elected governor is in place.

item.title: Caiado lança vídeo direcionado a mulheres e diz que tem mão pesada contra agressores
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, a PSD presidential pre-candidate, will release videos after Easter focusing on public security, including one targeting women on combating femicide. He emphasizes a 'heavy hand against aggressors,' reinforcing security as his main political banner.

item.title: Elmano e Ciro empatam em disputa pelo governo do CE, diz pesquisa
item.description: Elmano de Freitas (PT) and Ciro Gomes (PSDB) are tied in the race for governor of Ceará with 32% each in the first-round scenario. In a second-round scenario without Ciro, Elmano leads with 45%, followed by Roberto Cláudio (União Brasil) at 28%. The poll also shows 58% approval of Elmano’s governance, compared to 40% disapproval.

item.title: IA da CGU emitiu 35 mil alertas de risco em licitações federais
item.description: The CGU reported that its AI system ALICE issued 35,000 risk alerts in federal procurement between January 2023 and February 2026, during Lula’s government. Of these, 729 triggered audits, leading to 1,523 formal communications to officials. The CGU stresses the alerts are preventive, not proof of wrongdoing, and require technical validation to confirm irregularities.

item.title: Feminicídio mostra que falhamos em educar “macho”, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that femicide reveals a failure in educating boys, describing the 'macho' as violent and believing they own the truth, and emphasized that education must teach boys they are not superior or owners of women. He made the remarks during the delivery of 107 educational works across 16 states, stressing that quality education depends on student-teacher interaction, not just financial investment. The government also released data on school connectivity and infrastructure, reinforcing its commitment to improving public education.

item.title: Como Avenida Brasil fez Dilma Rousseff mudar agenda em 2012
item.description: In October 2012, the final episode of the telenovela Avenida Brasil generated such national impact that President Dilma Rousseff adjusted her official schedule to avoid conflicting with its broadcast. She postponed a rally in São Paulo where she was set to appear alongside Fernando Haddad, the PT’s candidate for mayor, during the 2012 municipal elections campaign. This episode illustrates the significant cultural influence of television on political agendas in Brazil.

item.title: Lindbergh pede a Moraes que Bolsonaro volte para a Papudinha
item.description: Federal Deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) urged Minister Alexandre de Moraes to revoke Jair Bolsonaro's house arrest and return him to closed regime at Papudinha, citing violations of precautionary measures after a video by Eduardo Bolsonaro at CPAC in the U.S. Bolsonaro's defense denies knowledge of the recording, while he remains under house arrest in Brasília recovering from bronchopneumonia. The petition notes the original ruling mandated immediate revocation upon any breach of conditions.

item.title: Valdemar elogia governos Lula com Alencar e critica gestão atual
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL, criticized the current Lula administration, stating that 'everything is going wrong' and highlighting the excessive number of 40 ministries as 'a barbarity'. He praised, however, Lula's first two terms (2003-2010) when José Alencar was vice-president, calling them a 'good government' with 'different investments'. Valdemar also argued that the current government must 'cut spending' and cited Bolsonaro's pandemic-era fund transfers to municipalities as an example of effective management, claiming Bolsonaro 'would have made a much better government' without the health crisis.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei que cria 17,8 mil cargos e gasto em 2026 chega a R$ 5,3 bilhões
item.description: President Lula signed a law creating 17,800 public sector positions, with 16,300 allocated to the Ministry of Education and 1,500 to the Ministry of Management and Innovation. The projected cost for 2026 is R$ 5.3 billion. The measure aims to strengthen state presence in key areas such as education and public administration.

item.title: Saiba o que Caiado diz sobre escala 6 X 1, Bolsa Família e reformas
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás and PSD pre-candidate for president in 2026, advocated in a Poder360 interview (Jan 21, 2026) for an administrative reform based on meritocracy and performance evaluations, criticized the fiscal framework as a 'farce' and the tax reform for risking income concentration in wealthier states. Regarding Bolsa Família, he said he would maintain it but make it temporary, tied to professional qualification and labor market insertion. On the 6x1 work scale, he stated it no longer exists in practice and that the focus should be on increasing worker income through productivity, not just reducing hours.

item.title: Escolhido para ser candidato do PSD, Caiado tenta emplacar terceira via
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado has been announced as the PSD's presidential candidate for the 2026 elections, aiming to position himself as a third way to break Brazil's political polarization. His candidacy seeks to offer an alternative to the two dominant ideological poles shaping the national electoral landscape.

item.title: PGR se manifesta contra afastamento de governador do Maranhão em ação movida pelo PCdoB
item.description: The PGR opposed the removal of Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão, stating there is insufficient evidence he violated STF rulings on nepotism, though it acknowledged the PCdoB's claims are serious and warrant investigation. The case stems from a 2024 Solidariedade action alleging 14 individuals tied to the governor were appointed to non-political public roles, a matter already ruled on by Justice Alexandre de Moraes, who ordered their dismissal. Subprosecutor Claudia Sampaio Marques emphasized that removing a governor requires incontrovertible proof and warned against using judicial proceedings as a political battleground.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro nega uso de celular em prisão domiciliar
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro denied that he violated the ban on cellphone use during house arrest, following claims that his son Eduardo Bolsonaro had filmed a video for him at a U.S. event. The Supreme Court requested explanations, as breaching house arrest conditions could result in his return to prison. Former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro also denied receiving any such material.

item.title: Com Caiado candidato, a direita despe a fantasia de ‘centro’ e tende a fechar com Flávio
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado’s 2026 presidential candidacy exposes the absence of a true center in Brazilian politics, revealing that the right is consolidating around figures aligned with bolsonarism, like himself. Though presented as a moderate option by Gilberto Kassab, Caiado is clearly right-wing and has a history of supporting Bolsonaro, making his candidacy an attempt to fake centrism that does not exist. His low polling and trajectory suggest he may eventually endorse Flávio Bolsonaro, abandoning the façade of independence.

item.title: Tarcísio diz que nova ideia de subvenção do diesel é ‘razoável’ e SP deve aderir à proposta
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas stated that the federal government's proposal to grant a R$1.20 per liter subsidy on imported diesel is 'reasonable' and that his state should adhere to the measure, although it is still under discussion. He noted the cost would be split equally between the Union (R$0.60) and the states (R$0.60), and argued the proposal is viable because the federal government can offset revenue losses through other fiscal tools, unlike states that rely on ICMS and cannot forgo this revenue without breaching the Fiscal Responsibility Law.

item.title: “Caiado é um conservador legítimo”, diz Otoni de Paula
item.description: Federal deputy Otoni de Paula, who left the MDB to join the PSD, says his goal is to help former Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado, a 2026 presidential pre-candidate, connect with the evangelical community. Otoni claims he previously brought President Lula (PT) closer to evangelicals and describes Caiado as a 'legitimate conservative'.

item.title: PGR pede que Moraes rejeite afastamento de governador desafeto de Dino no Maranhão
item.description: The PGR urged Justice Alexandre de Moraes to reject a request to remove Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão, citing lack of incontestable proof that he violated STF orders on nepotism. Brandão’s defense denies wrongdoing and accuses Flávio Dino’s political group of exploiting the process for political-electoral gains, while Dino’s allies claim the governor failed to comply with orders to suspend three aides over nepotism. The case unfolds amid political and legal battles in Maranhão, ahead of the deadline for officials to step down before running in the 2026 elections.

item.title: PSD bate o martelo e escolhe Ronaldo Caiado como pré-candidato à presidência
item.description: The PSD has officially nominated Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás, as its presidential pre-candidate for 2026, following the withdrawal of Ratinho Junior. Caiado pledged to pacify the country through a broad amnesty for those involved in the January 8 attacks, including former president Jair Bolsonaro. While Ratinho Junior praised the choice, Eduardo Leite of Rio Grande do Sul criticized it for deepening national polarization.

item.title: Malafaia cita avião 'bem velho' e diz que está 'orando a Deus' para que toque pessoas: 'Preciso de um mais novo'
item.description: Pastor Silas Malafaia defended replacing his 1985 aircraft, calling it a 'work tool' and saying he is praying to God for a newer one to 'reach people', in response to criticism over church resource use. He confirmed an informal meeting with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro in São Paulo, with no explicit electoral support negotiation, reiterating he sees figures like Tarcísio de Freitas and Michelle Bolsonaro as more competitive for 2026, but leaves open the possibility of supporting whoever consolidates in the conservative field.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei que estrutura carreiras e reajusta salários de servidores; impacto será de R$ 5,3 bi
item.description: President Lula signed a law restructuring federal public service careers and adjusting salaries, with an estimated budgetary impact of R$ 5.3 billion. The measure affects 200,000 active and retired servants, creating positions in education, health, justice, and management, and updating the Efficiency and Productivity Bonus for retirees. Part of the impact depends on the implementation of institutes and competitive exams, and may not be fully executed in 2026.

item.title: Lula nomeia Carlos Fávaro de volta para Ministério da Agricultura
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has reappointed Carlos Fávaro as Minister of Agriculture and Livestock, as published in an extra edition of the Official Gazette of the Union on Monday, the 30th. This appointment reinforces the continuity of the federal government's agricultural policy.

item.title: Promessa de anistia a Bolsonaro na estreia de Caiado incomoda campanha de Flávio por possível impacto na base
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado’s promise of amnesty in his campaign launch irritated Flávio Bolsonaro’s team, as it signals a direct bid for the same Bolsonarist electorate. Despite the discomfort, Flávio’s allies argue for maintaining a strategy focused on economic agenda and alliances, avoiding a narrative clash for now. The prevailing view is that Caiado may act as a 'side line' — taking on the ideological fight against Lula — thereby allowing Flávio to pursue a more calibrated approach.

item.title: Erika Hilton pede que AGU adote medidas contra Flávio por ataques a processo eleitoral
item.description: Federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSOL) has urged the AGU to take action against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) for undermining the legitimacy of the 2022 Brazilian election by claiming foreign interference without evidence. She argues this constitutes an abuse of parliamentary mandate, spreads disinformation threatening democracy, and demands accountability, retraction, and platform intervention.

item.title: Quem é Ronaldo Caiado, escolhido pelo PSD para ser candidato a presidente
item.description: The governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, has been selected by the PSD as its presidential candidate for the 2026 election. A physician and ruralist leader with over three decades in public office, Caiado draws strong support from agribusiness and promotes a conservative agenda centered on public safety and criticism of PT governments. The decision sparked internal dissent, with Rio Grande do Sul’s governor Eduardo Leite criticizing the move as deepening polarization, while Paraná’s governor Ratinho Júnior endorsed it as a democratic commitment.

item.title: Lula lidera corrida presidencial no Ceará, diz pesquisa
item.description: Lula leads the presidential race in Ceará with 59% of voting intentions in two scenarios tested by RealTime BigData, while Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) trails with 22-23%. Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) was included in the poll after announcing his pre-candidacy, but Eduardo Leite (PSD) is not expected to run. Lula has 64% approval and 37% rejection, compared to 58% rejection for Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Lula sanciona nesta terça projeto que amplia licença paternidade
item.description: President Lula signed into law on Tuesday a bill expanding paternity leave in Brazil, increasing it from 5 to 20 days, with the possibility of extension up to 30 days in cases of adoption or birth of a child with a disability. The measure aims to encourage paternal involvement in early childcare and promote greater gender equality in the family and labor market.

item.title: Esquerda critica Flávio Bolsonaro por fala sobre terras-raras nos EUA
item.description: Left-wing politicians and members of Lula's government criticized Flávio Bolsonaro for stating that Brazil could be a solution to reduce U.S. dependence on China for rare earths and critical minerals during his speech at CPAC in the U.S. They accused him of treason, offering Brazil's strategic resources in exchange for foreign support, and spreading lies about the country, calling his remarks the 'most serious fact' in the 2026 elections so far.

item.title: Filipe Martins pede ao STF autorização para receber visita de Moro, Nikolas entre outros
item.description: Filipe Martins, former international affairs advisor to the Presidency, requested STF permission to receive visits from 16 opposition parliamentarians, including Sérgio Moro and Nikolas Ferreira, for reserved dialogue. Minister Alexandre de Moraes had already authorized initial visits from two state legislators. Martins has been detained since January 2026 for allegedly violating a social media ban, with LinkedIn access detected in December 2025.

item.title: Caiado: “Ganhar do PT é fácil, o difícil é governar para que não volte”
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, PSD pre-candidate for president, said defeating the PT in elections is easy, but the real challenge is governing efficiently to prevent the party from returning to power. He emphasized public safety, lowering production costs, and boosting innovation, citing Goiás’ success in eliminating bank robberies and reducing crimes like the 'new cangaço'. Caiado warned Brazil is losing ground in strategic sectors like industry and energy, and stressed the need for a strong state to combat organized crime and prepare the country to compete in AI and clean energy.

item.title: André de Paula assume Ministério da Agricultura na 4ª feira
item.description: André de Paula (PSD) will assume the Ministry of Agriculture on Wednesday, April 1st, 2026, replacing Carlos Fávaro, who is leaving the post to run for Senate re-election in Mato Grosso. The handover ceremony will take place at Embrapa's auditorium in Brasília at 3:00 PM. Rivetla Edipo, the executive secretary of Fisheries, will temporarily lead the Ministry of Fisheries and Aquaculture, as part of President Lula's strategy to keep various ministries under executive secretaries during the ministerial reform.

item.title: Janja defende PL da Misoginia e rebate Nikolas: “Mente e distorce”
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva countered criticism from federal deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG), stating that those who lie and distort information about Bill 896/2023 — which adds misogyny to the Racism Law — ignore the ongoing killings of women by men in Brazil. She emphasized that while Ferreira attacks the bill, gender-based violence persists, referencing femicide cases from the past weekend. Janja urged swift approval of the bill to protect women's lives.

item.title: “Ninguém está autorizado a usar meu nome”, diz Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro declared that no one is authorized to use his name in public statements, following criticism for his speech at CPAC in the U.S., where he positioned Brazil as a supplier of critical minerals to the U.S., compared Jair Bolsonaro’s legal situation to Donald Trump’s, and accused Lula of lobbying to prevent PCC and Comando Vermelho from being designated terrorist organizations. Government allies condemned his remarks as treasonous and strategically damaging to national sovereignty.

item.title: Caiado, sobre anistia: quando há proposta no plano de governo, passa a ser plebiscito
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) stated that a potential victory in the 2026 elections could be interpreted as a popular plebiscite on granting amnesty to those convicted for the January 8 events, including former President Jair Bolsonaro. He links the legitimacy of his potential election to public approval of such an amnesty measure.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro nega contato com Eduardo e diz que cumpre regras ‘de forma rigorosa’
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro denied any direct or indirect contact with the former president during his house arrest, stating he was unaware of a video recorded by his son Eduardo Bolsonaro at CPAC in the U.S. Lawyers asserted Bolsonaro is strictly complying with imposed conditions, including bans on communication devices and video recording, and that the video was a third-party statement without his involvement. The defense submitted clarifications to the STF following a request by Minister Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: Ao contrário da esquerda, a extrema-direita se movimenta rapidamente — até no exterior
item.description: The far-right is mobilizing rapidly, even internationally, with coordinated actions at events like the summit with Latin American leaders, while the left remains slow and disorganized. The author criticizes historical allies of the far-right, such as the military and Zionists, pointing to a pattern of repression and alliances with foreign powers reminiscent of the 1964 coup. Despite the grim outlook, he places hope in the Brazilian people, who resist and rise even without being called to politics.

item.title: Análise: 1º discurso de Caiado abre fosso do pré-candidato do PSD com Flávio Bolsonaro e o bolsonarismo
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado's first speech as PSD's pre-candidate for president signals a clear break from Flávio Bolsonaro and bolsonarism, positioning himself outside the Lula-Bolsonaro polarization. He criticizes polarization, advocates for moderation, science, and effective governance, arguing that the right must replicate successful state-level management models (e.g., Goiás, São Paulo) rather than rely on political inheritance. Caiado also implies that the Bolsonaro government failed to consolidate power, and that the PT only loses relevance when the right governs well.

item.title: Pré-candidato do PSD, Caiado acena para o agro e critica políticas do PT
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, PSD's pre-candidate for president, emphasized his long-standing defense of agribusiness since 1986, criticizing PT's interventionist policies and advocating for free markets and technological innovation as drivers of national development. He pledged to scale Goiás' agro-modernization policies—including tech investment and rare earth exploration—to the national level.

item.title: Lula faz crítica indireta a PowerPoint da “GloboNews” sobre Banco Master
item.description: President Lula indirectly criticized GloboNews for displaying an incomplete diagram about the Banco Master case, stating that the media will not show all the actions of his government. He noted that the presentation, which listed officials allegedly linked to Banco Master founder Daniel Vorcaro, was deemed inadequate and prompted GloboNews to issue a public apology, acknowledging the material was wrong and incomplete.

item.title: Análise: Caiado se lança de olho no eleitor de direita não bolsonarista e critica Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado launched his presidential pre-candidacy for the PSD, positioning himself as a right-wing alternative for conservative voters dissatisfied with radicalized bolsonarism. He directly attacked Flávio Bolsonaro with a medical analogy questioning his management capacity and defended broad amnesty to consolidate conservative support. Caiado stated that defeating Lula at the polls is 'easy,' but the real challenge is governing in a way that makes the PT irrelevant in the long term.

item.title: Caiado diz que usará ‘prerrogativas’ para anistiar Bolsonaro mesmo com posição contrária do STF
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) stated he will use his 'prerogatives' to grant amnesty to Jair Bolsonaro, even if the Supreme Federal Court (STF) opposes it, claiming the measure reflects the will of the majority. He said amnesty would be one of his first actions if elected and that he will not relinquish his constitutional powers, even if they conflict with the Judiciary. Caiado was officially selected by the PSD as its presidential candidate following Ratinho Júnior's withdrawal.

item.title: Otoni se filia ao PSD e diz que vai ajudar Caiado chegar aos evangélicos
item.description: Federal deputy and pastor Otoni de Paula left the MDB and joined the PSD on Monday (30), with the mission of helping presidential candidate Ronaldo Caiado connect with the evangelical community. The party switch is part of a strategy to broaden religious support in Caiado's 2026 election campaign.

item.title: Lula anuncia Leonardo Barchini como novo ministro da Educação
item.description: President Lula announced Leonardo Barchini, the current executive secretary of the Ministry of Education, as the new education minister, replacing Camilo Santana, who will leave the post to run in the October elections. The appointment reflects Lula’s strategy of ensuring continuity by promoting experienced internal officials. During the event, the government delivered 107 educational works across 16 states, funded by R$ 413.49 million from the Novo PAC and MEC.

item.title: Bolsonaro não teve acesso a vídeo de Eduardo, diz defesa do ex-presidente a Moraes
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro told Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes that Bolsonaro had no access to or participation in the video recorded by his son, former federal deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro, in the United States. The statement was made in response to a STF order requiring clarification on Eduardo’s claims about the video. The defense denied any direct involvement by the former president in the production or dissemination of the material.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado descarta compor com Flávio Bolsonaro e diz que PSD garantiu candidatura
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado ruled out forming a ticket with Flávio Bolsonaro, stating his move to the PSD was conditioned on the party guaranteeing him a presidential candidacy. He emphasized he is not seeking any other office, such as the Senate, and switched parties solely to viable his presidential run. The comments come amid speculation about right-wing realignments, including the possible Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema joining the PSD as a running mate for Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Lula anuncia novo ministro da Educação e rebate críticos: 'Vamos construir nossas próprias notícias'
item.description: Lula announced that Leonardo Barchini will become the new Minister of Education, replacing Camilo Santana, who is stepping down to support Elmano de Freitas' re-election campaign in Ceará. During a ceremony inaugurating 107 education projects nationwide, Lula urged government workers to spread the administration's achievements, declaring, 'we will build our own news' to counter misinformation. Barchini, a trusted aide of Santana and close to Haddad and Padilha, previously served as Haddad’s chief of staff in São Paulo and held roles in the MEC during Lula’s and Dilma’s administrations.

item.title: Com a escolha de Caiado na corrida à Presidência, qual será o papel de Eduardo Leite nas eleições?
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul and former PSD presidential pre-candidate for 2026, was sidelined after the party chose Ronaldo Caiado as its nominee to face Gilberto Kassab. Leite avoided directly commenting on Caiado, stated his role remains to be defined by the group, and affirmed he will remain governor until 2027, ruling out a Senate bid.

item.title: Convocado a decidir politicamente, STF prepara eleição-tampão direta no Rio
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Federal Court (STF) is being called upon to make a political decision regarding the supplementary election for governor of Rio de Janeiro, following Cláudio Castro’s resignation to avoid electoral impeachment. The goal is to prevent Douglas Ruas, a Bolsonarist-right candidate aligned with Castro, from assuming office until October, thereby favoring Eduardo Paes, who would benefit from a direct election scheduled for June. The article frames this as a partisan legal maneuver to cleanse the state of a 'criminal command' and restore democratic order.

item.title: O xerife e o herdeiro: Caiado na corrida eleitoral põe 2x1 da direita contra Lula
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado's PSD presidential pre-candidacy creates a 2x1 dynamic on the right, positioning him as a pragmatic, institutional alternative alongside Flávio Bolsonaro, who mobilizes the bolsonarist base. Despite low first-round voting intention (3.6%), Caiado’s low rejection and strong governance image in public safety make him a potential aggregator of center-right votes, especially among the middle class and productive sectors. His 'Goiás model' contrasts with Flávio’s visceral style, but PT strategists bet on right-wing fragmentation to benefit Lula.
